description: A batter-based cooked dish made in a patterned waffle iron until crisp on the outside and tender inside. Waffles are commonly eaten for breakfast or brunch, and sweeter styles are also served as desserts with toppings such as butter, syrup, fruit, or whipped cream.
core_ingredients: Leavening such as baking powder or yeast
preparation_steps: Prepare the batter by mixing dry ingredients and wet ingredients, then combining them without overmixing.
Preheat and lightly grease the waffle iron if needed.
Pour batter into the waffle iron and cook until golden brown and crisp.
Remove carefully and serve plain or with desired toppings.
For yeasted styles such as some Belgian waffles, allow the batter to rest or ferment before cooking.

nutrition.calories: Usually moderate to high per serving, depending on toppings

health_advice.benefits: Provides quick energy and can be made more balanced when served with fruit, yogurt, or nut butter.
health_advice.risks: Can become high in sugar, refined carbohydrates, and calories when served with heavy syrups, cream, or dessert toppings.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use moderate toppings and consider whole grain batter, less added sugar, and fruit-based accompaniments.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional waffles usually contain wheat flour, milk, eggs, and butter. Vegan and gluten-free versions are possible with suitable substitutions.

allergens: Possible soy depending on batter mix or toppings
